calendar.matters:
Motion To Request Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Jun-28-2010 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Mar-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eugene Schneider in Propria Persona.

Mr. Schneider stated that the Substition of Attorney had been sent to his Client's and that it was returned by Mr. Vann with the box checked in error indicating that the Parties were representing themselves. In addition, Mr. Schneider stated that it had been returned to Mr. Vann on February 10th and that he had not heard from Mr. Vann or the Clients since then. The Court found good cause to grant the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). Eugene Schneider is relieved as Attorney for Patricia Jackson and Marian Jackson-Chavez.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR#13358. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-16-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eugene Schneider for and with Patricia Jackson and Marian Jackson-Chavez (Petitioners).

The Court stated that the issue before the Court was the request for extraordinary attorney fees. Patricia Jackson stated that the declaration filed December 8, 2008 outlined the petitioner's position regarding Mr. Schnieder's request for extraordinary fees and noted that he should only receive one-third of the amount he was requesting. Mr. Schneider presented argument to support his position and stated that his request was reasonable. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-10-2008 Continued To Dec-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eugene Schneider for and with Patricia Jackson and Marian Chavez (Co-Executors).

Mr. Schneider stated that a series of consents and waivers related to the supplement, the accounting and consent to serve had been signed. In addition, he informed the Court that he was missing one signature from the Beneficiary residing in Modesto as he was unable to reach her and that the supplement had not been signed and verified by the Co-Executors. The Court informed Mr. Schneider that the supplement needed to be signed and served unless the Co-Executors signed a waiver. Ms. Jackson stated that the matter had been going on for some time now and requested the Court review appendix F of the petition. The matter was continued to December 15, 2008 for the issue regarding the extraordinary fee request.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Murphy.

Petition Of Interested Person Under P.C. 9614 Re : Estate Representatives Distribution Of Petitioner'S Non-Probate Assets In Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Merchant (appearing for Mr. Schneider) for Marian Jackson-Chavez and Patricia Jackson. Robert Ferris for Rose Jackson.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court found that the amount of $250,045.00 needed to be paid by the Estate. The Court will review the order presented by Mr. Ferris. The Court directed Mr. Ferris to amend the Petition For Final Distribution taking today's ruling into consideration and to serve notice.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Mann.

Petition Of Interested Person Under P.C. 9614 Re : Estate Representatives Distribution Of Petitioner'S Non-Probate Assets In Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-29-2008 Continued To Oct-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for Rose Jackson (Petitioner). Eugene Schneider for Patricia Alice Jackson and Marian Jackson Chavez.

The Court stated that this wasn't a 9614 petition, it is actually opposition to the upcoming November petition for final distribution, and it should be an 850 petition. Mr. Ferris requested that this petition be heard before the November date for final distribution. Mr. Schneider noted that his clients were not personally served. The Court continued the matter to October 14, 2008, and instructed Mr. Ferris to give proper notice to all parties in the estate and to notice anyone interested in the estate. In addition, the Court gave Mr. Schneider until the close of business on Wedenesday, October 8, 2008, to file and serve his opposition. The Court further noted that it was treating the petition as a 9614 petition; as filed by counsel.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/ R.Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2637-2639 22nd St., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Jul-27-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Eugene Schneider appearing for petitioner. Reappraisal for sale was filed this morning. The buyer is in court. Waivers of notice filed. All procedural problems are resolved. Terms of sale stated by counsel. Counsel corrected the amount of the first overbid. Bid was increased to $1,120,000.00. N/B. Counsel will prepare the order.
Presiding: Dorothy L. McMath, Judge Pro Tem. Reporter: Dave Davenport. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Handa.
